mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding household income and consumption demand, which translate into higher expected corporate revenues and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment growth precedes consumption expenditures with a short lag
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 3.0% reflects a sufficiently restrictive monetary stance that raises corporate discount rates and debt-service burdens, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 3.04% in June 2005 after sustained tightening
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over any 2-year window is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated retail sales (RSAFS) and low unemployment (UNRATE) indicate demand-driven corporate revenue growth without immediate labor-market bottlenecks, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond the sum of individual effects.
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to outperform the linear combination of individual signals in directional accuracy, or the interaction term in a logistic regression carries a negative coefficient with p-value < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days specifically during monetary tightening cycles, because the Federal Reserve is unwilling or unable to offset supply weakness with rate cuts, amplifying downside equity risk.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%, or the signal Rank IC < 0.03 in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth surprises coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 2000 thousand units signal an overheated residential construction sector that is increasingly vulnerable to monetary tightening and mean reversion, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as systemic risk accumulates.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST printed 2009 thousand units two months prior to decision date
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when HOUST exceeds 2000, or the signal's information coefficient is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window signals weakening aggregate demand and incoming corporate earnings downgrades, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.2 percentage points coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the triggered return distribution is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures consumer spending momentum, the largest component of aggregate demand, and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
